nodes.x: Then Job answered and said,
Then Job answered and said,
But Job answered and said,
And Job answered and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

external  sense  to  be  abundantly  occupied  with  pains,  but  it 
must  also  be  full  of  groaning.  Now  groaning  is  a  violent  and 
intense  pain.  For  we
Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
By reason of the voice of my groaning my bones cleave to my skin.
If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:

NEU.                   Your good mother!
She has already had so much to suffer.
Must this last heaviest stroke too fall on her?
Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!

"’Oh!  Lord Vancome!  So you know him. Where did you meet him?’  Then
silently, ’_I wonder if he came out so as to walk back with me?_’
And I knew such a man, (whether in the body, or out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth;)

Just as I got to the gate at the end of the lane, I met him riding over
to take tea with us.
I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.

And I will plead against him with pestilence and with blood; and I will rain upon him, and upon his bands, and upon the many people that are with him, an overflowing rain, and great hailstones, fire, and brimstone.
Listen to him who is the advocate with the Father, who is pleading your cause before him—

3. Listen to Him who is the Advocate with the Father, who is
    pleading your cause before Him,
For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name’s sake.
I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.
And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
O that he would rid you from this iniquity and abomination. And, O that ye would listen unto the word of his commands, and let not this pride of your hearts destroy your souls!
And he said unto them, What manner of man was he which came up to meet you, and told you these words?
I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.

And if he will offer unto me an acceptable offering, and acknowledgments, and remain with my people, behold, I, the Lord your God, will heal him that he shall be healed; and he shall lift up his voice again on the mountains, and be a spokesman before my face.
Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.
If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?
And I will give unto him power to be mighty in testimony.
Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.
Or let him take hold of my strength, that he may make peace with me; and he shall make peace with me.
Wherefore will ye plead with me? ye all have transgressed against me, saith the Lord.
There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, but I would make supplication to my judge.
And I charged your judges at that time, saying, Hear the causes between your brethren, and judge righteously between every man and his brother, and the stranger that is with him.
Then hear thou in heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way upon his head; and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.
And said to the judges, Take heed what ye do: for ye judge not for man, but for the Lord, who is with you in the judgment.
For all his judgments were before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me.
Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:
On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:

seen by any one either in heaven or on earth? How could He be said to go - from one place to another, if He were always in one place as much as in another ? How could He move at' all, in any direction, if He were in all parts of His dominions alike and at once. The idea of His Son being with Him, or of angels worshipping before Him, or of heaven being His dwelling-^ce, or of His glorified Saints…
Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.
I say unto you, he hath seen him; nevertheless, he who came unto his own was not comprehended.
And it came to pass that he said unto me: Look! And I looked as if to look upon him, and I saw him not; for he had gone from before my presence.
On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:
Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:
And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.
He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.

He presides over the worlds on worlds that illuminate this little
planet, and millions on millions of worlds that we cannot see; and yet
he looks upon the minutest object of his creations; not one of these
creatures escapes his notice; and there is not one of them but his
wisdom and power has produced. 1:39.
But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.
But if he thus say, I have no delight in thee; behold, here am I, let him do to me as seemeth good unto him.
Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
He that seeketh me early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.

Yea, ye will lift him up, and ye will give unto him of your substance; ye will give unto him of your gold, and of your silver, and ye will clothe him with costly apparel; and because he speaketh flattering words unto you, and he saith that all is well, then ye will not find fault with him.
And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.
If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;
If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.
Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat.
And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

I said to him, “ Ask himself, sir, whether from 
the time that he has entered my house I have 
done anything improper, or have offended him 
in any respect.” He answered, “I also know 
that you neither have done nor will do anything 
improper, and therefore I speak these words to 
you, that you may persevere. For he had a good 
report of you to me, and you will say these 
words to others, that…
But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.
Then shall his mind change, and he shall pass over, and offend, imputing this his power unto his god.
But he that is joined unto the Lord is one spirit.
And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.
For I will counsel him concerning this matter, and all things whatsoever he shall appoint unto them shall be fulfilled.
me voice of command, and nothing encouraged in his faithfulness, will comply from necessity and fear, if at all, and not from a sense of duty, pleasure or respect.
Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
And for this cause I said that he is a wicked man, for he has sought to take away the things wherewith you have been entrusted; and he has also sought to destroy your gift.
And this is the beginning of the stewardship which I have appointed unto him, for a blessing upon him, and upon his father.
Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.
Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.
For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:
The troubles of my heart are enlarged: O bring thou me out of my distresses.
My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.

¶ Mine heart within me is broken because of the prophets; all my bones shake; I am like a drunken man, and like a man whom wine hath overcome, because of the Lord, and because of the words of his holiness.
Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?
Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.
He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.
And I, God, saw the light; and that light was good. And I, God, divided the light from the darkness.
If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.
I am the light which shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not.
